data.body:
PASS 1 — T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe) on the workflow-engine remediation epic.

VERDICT: clean for T8. The LLM mechanisms this epic concretely asserts are structurally complete (referenced enums/protocols/fallbacks are defined and tool-verified):
- Three-pass gating pattern (Pass-1 evidence → Pass-2 binary-verify → Pass-3 deterministic gate) is named and grounded; the finding schema + verifier listing are co-located and canonical in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py (verify_finding_listing / verify_instructions — "the ONE canonical format").
- ISF ("intent-source fidelity") criterion: trigger (fires only when ticket is LINKED to a session log), model tier (frontier/Opus), step structure (2-step extract→check), and FALLBACK are all specified — not-applicable/skip when no linked intent source (confirmed criteria_routing.json:1177), and a deterministic pre-retrieval step for oversized session logs (summarize_for_isf exists, passes.py:350). No undefined-but-referenced enum.
- Eval-metric vocabulary (recall / false-accept / false-fire) and the three corpuses (DSO read-only, snap-oakhart-manual, rebar's own tickets) match established experiment-corpus usage.
- Agent-vs-single-turn bright line and the prompt-engineering do/don't list are fully specified vocabularies, not dangling references.
- Pink-elephant / instruction-locality antipatterns are explicitly addressed (affirmative anti-FP framing), satisfying the locality checklist item.

NON-T8 observation (flagged for the relevant pass, not raised as a T8 finding): WS-CLEANUP's "current inventory" lists examples/code_review.yaml + templates.py as pending-removal, but the tree shows these are already removed (examples/review_skeleton.yaml:11 calls them "the throwaway examples/code_review.yaml + the templates.py" already cleaned up). Stale inventory — accuracy issue, not an LLM structural gap.
